hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance complementarity with SSTR2-specific ECL2 (res 192/193/195/197) and TM5 (205/208/209/212) while avoiding off-target SSTR3/SSTR5 interactions. Simultaneously, mutating position 6 to acidic residues (E/D) will introduce electrostatic complementarity with SSTR2-unique TM6 (272/273/276/279) and disrupt off-target interactions.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs achieve strong SSTR2 binding but lack selectivity due to conserved interactions at non-pharmacophore positions. Targeted mutations at these positions will improve selectivity without compromising on-target affinity.

proposed_changes.rationale: Target non-pharmacophore positions that are likely involved in off-target interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5. These positions can be mutated to disrupt conserved-pocket contacts while preserving the pharmacophore (FWKT(7-10) and Cys3/Cys14).

Introduce mutations at non-pharmacophore positions to disrupt conserved interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5. Acidic residues at position 1 and basic residues at position 2 can help repel off-target interactions, while small hydrophobic residues at positions 5, 6, 11, and 12 can reduce hydrophobic contacts with off-targets.
